title: 507 Hectares, 12 Fabs: Inside TSMC's Largest Taiwan Expansion Yet
author: J
text:
K

karin623
Member

In southern Taiwan, TSMC is quietly preparing what could become the largest fab expansion project in its history: a 507-hectare campus with plans for as many as 12 fabs, potentially dedicated to 1.4nm and even 1nm production.

The project is larger than TSMC's Arizona site and could define the company's Taiwan footprint for the next decade.

Yet building fabs is only half the story. The real challenge lies in navigating environmental reviews, ecological preservation, and the competing priorities of a society trying to balance semiconductor leadership with sustainability.

The battle over Shalun may ultimately reveal how far Taiwan is willing to go to keep TSMC's future anchored at home.

507 Hectares, 12 Fabs: Inside TSMC's Largest Taiwan Expansion Yet

Liang-rong Chen

cwnewsroom.substack.com
